---
title: "LRPITT15b: Taught vs not-taught receptive vocabulary (generalisation contrast)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT15b is the *receptive*-modality companion to LRPITT15: the within-data test
of "gains are largest in directly-taught skills, with little generalisation". Two
Block 1 receptive vocabulary outcomes are modelled jointly over the randomised
window (t1 $\rightarrow$ t2):

- `TR` — directly-taught target words (`b1retau`, $N = 24$);
- `UR` — not-taught comparison words (`b1rent`, $N = 12$).

The headline quantity is $\tau_{\mathrm{TR}} - \tau_{\mathrm{UR}}$ — *benefit on
taught minus benefit on not-taught*. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the
intervention arm, so positive $\tau$ is a benefit; a clearly positive difference
means the programme raised the taught words more than untaught words (limited
transfer). The per-outcome models are LRPITT01 (TR) and LRPITT03 (UR).

## Structure

For child $i$ and outcome $k \in \{\mathrm{TR}, \mathrm{UR}\}$:

$$
\eta_{k,i} = \alpha_k + \tau_k G_i
           + \gamma^{\text{own}}_k\, \text{logit}_*(k_{\text{pre},i}, N_k)
           + \gamma^{A}_k\, A_{\text{std},i},
$$

with a per-outcome Beta-Binomial likelihood and dispersion $\kappa_k$. As in
LRPITT15, the 2×2 LKJ residual correlation is **off**: the within-child residual
SD sits at ~0 once baselines are conditioned on, so the block only destabilised
the variance component for no gain. Dropping it is the conservative choice and
converges cleanly.

::: {.callout-note title="Denominators and caveats"}

- Taught ($\mathrm{TR}$): $N = 24$ — documented (Burgoyne et al., 2012, Table 3).
- Not-taught ($\mathrm{UR}$): $N = 12$ — **observed maximum, not documented** and
  flagged unconfirmed; the logit-scale difference is robust but the
  probability-scale interpretation of $\mathrm{UR}$ is approximate.
- $n \approx 54$; the causal claim is limited to the randomised window. The
  difference is a *differential logit-scale effect*, not a difference in raw words.
- The difference is conservative with respect to the (near-zero) within-child
  residual correlation; `use_residual_correlation=True` remains available as a
  sensitivity fit but mixes poorly here.

:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_joint.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
